source_paper_title: Robust Sparsification for Matroid Intersection with Applications
context:
Page 6, end of the "Density-Based Decomposition and Principal Partitions" discussion in Section 1 (Introduction), paragraph beginning "Although the above procedure can be costly..."

Moreover, in our algorithms, as we frequently update the ground set on which we compute the decomposition by adding or removing one element, there may be room to improve our time complexity: we leave as an open question whether updating a density-based decomposition when performing these kinds of operations can be done more efficiently, without re-computing the whole decomposition each time.
title: Efficiently update a matroid density-based decomposition under single-element insertions/deletions
background_latex: Let \(M=(V,\mathcal I)\) be a matroid with rank function \(\mathrm{rank}_M:2^V\to \mathbb Z_{\ge 0}\). For \(U\subseteq V\), define the density \(\rho_M(U)=|U|/\mathrm{rank}_M(U)\), with \(\rho_M(\emptyset)=0\). For \(V'\subseteq V\), let \(M|_{V'}\) denote the restriction of \(M\) to \(V'\). A density-based decomposition of \(V'\) in \(M|_{V'}\) is a sequence \(U_1,\dots,U_k\) (where \(k=\mathrm{rank}_M(V)\)) obtained by the greedy procedure: \(U_1\) is a maximum-density subset of \(V'\) in \(M|_{V'}\) (breaking ties by maximum cardinality), then \(U_2\) is a maximum-density subset in the contracted matroid \((M|_{V'})/U_1\), and so on until \(\bigcup_{i=1}^k U_i=V'\) (allowing empty sets at the end). Assume access to \(M\) via an independence or rank oracle, and that densest subsets can be computed from scratch in polynomial time (e.g., via submodular function minimization). Consider a dynamic process where \(V'\) is updated by inserting an element \(u\in V\setminus V'\) (forming \(V'\cup\{u\}\)) or deleting an element \(u\in V'\) (forming \(V'\setminus\{u\}\)).
problem_definition_latex: Design an algorithm that maintains the density-based decomposition \(U_1,\dots,U_k\) of \(V'\) in \(M|_{V'}\) under single-element insertions and deletions to \(V'\), with update time asymptotically faster than recomputing the entire decomposition from scratch after each update (e.g., achieving polylogarithmic or otherwise subpolynomial dependence on \(|V|\) per update while remaining polynomial in \(k\) and oracle costs), and prove the claimed update-time bound.
latex:
Let \(M=(V,\mathcal I)\) be a matroid with rank function \(\mathrm{rank}_M:2^V\to \mathbb Z_{\ge 0}\). For \(U\subseteq V\), define the density \(\rho_M(U)=|U|/\mathrm{rank}_M(U)\), with \(\rho_M(\emptyset)=0\). For \(V'\subseteq V\), let \(M|_{V'}\) denote the restriction of \(M\) to \(V'\). A density-based decomposition of \(V'\) in \(M|_{V'}\) is a sequence \(U_1,\dots,U_k\) (where \(k=\mathrm{rank}_M(V)\)) obtained by the greedy procedure: \(U_1\) is a maximum-density subset of \(V'\) in \(M|_{V'}\) (breaking ties by maximum cardinality), then \(U_2\) is a maximum-density subset in the contracted matroid \((M|_{V'})/U_1\), and so on until \(\bigcup_{i=1}^k U_i=V'\) (allowing empty sets at the end). Assume access to \(M\) via an independence or rank oracle, and that densest subsets can be computed from scratch in polynomial time (e.g., via submodular function minimization). Consider a dynamic process where \(V'\) is updated by inserting an element \(u\in V\setminus V'\) (forming \(V'\cup\{u\}\)) or deleting an element \(u\in V'\) (forming \(V'\setminus\{u\}\)).

Design an algorithm that maintains the density-based decomposition \(U_1,\dots,U_k\) of \(V'\) in \(M|_{V'}\) under single-element insertions and deletions to \(V'\), with update time asymptotically faster than recomputing the entire decomposition from scratch after each update (e.g., achieving polylogarithmic or otherwise subpolynomial dependence on \(|V|\) per update while remaining polynomial in \(k\) and oracle costs), and prove the claimed update-time bound.
